homework.title: Coulomb's Law Application
learningOutcomeIds: Apply Coulomb's law to calculate force between point charges
homework.instructions: Two point charges q1 = +2 μC and q2 = -4 μC are placed 0.5 meters apart in air. Calculate the magnitude and direction of the electrostatic force acting on each charge.
homework.question: Calculate the electrostatic force between two point charges of +2 μC and -4 μC separated by a distance of 0.5 m in air.
homework.answerSpace: Show all steps including formula substitution and unit conversion.
homework.visualRequirement: Draw a diagram showing the two charges, their positions, and the direction of the force vectors acting on them.

homework.title: Properties of Electric Charge
learningOutcomeIds: Explain the fundamental properties of electric charge
homework.instructions: Answer the following questions based on the properties of electric charge.
homework.question: a) State the principle of conservation of charge. b) Can a body have a charge of 0.5e? Justify your answer.
homework.answerSpace: Write concise answers with relevant examples.
homework.expectedResponse: a) Total charge in an isolated system remains constant. b) No, because charge is quantized (q = ne where n is an integer).

homework.title: Electric Field Lines
learningOutcomeIds: Draw and interpret electric field lines
homework.instructions: Draw the electric field lines for a dipole (two equal and opposite charges separated by a small distance). Label the regions of strong and weak fields.
homework.question: Sketch the pattern of electric field lines for an electric dipole. Mark the direction of the field at any point on the axial line.
homework.answerSpace: Provide a clear, labelled diagram with arrows indicating direction.
homework.visualRequirement: Draw a diagram showing two charges (+q and -q) and the curved field lines emerging from positive and entering negative charge. Include at least 3 field lines on each side.
homework.expectedResponse: Diagram showing curved lines from + to -, denser near charges, straight lines on axis.

homework.title: Electric Flux and Gauss's Law
learningOutcomeIds: Understand the concept of electric flux
homework.instructions: Read the assertion and reason carefully. Select the correct option.
homework.question: Assertion (A): The net electric flux through a closed surface enclosing no charge is zero. Reason (R): Electric field lines enter and leave a closed surface in equal numbers if no charge is enclosed inside.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
homework.answerSpace: Select the correct option and write a one-line justification.
homework.expectedResponse: Option 1. Both are true, and R correctly explains why flux is zero.

homework.title: Static Electricity in Daily Life
learningOutcomeIds: Connect theoretical concepts to real-world phenomena
homework.instructions: Explain the phenomenon of static electricity using the concept of charge transfer. Provide one real-life example.
homework.question: Why does a comb rubbed with dry hair attract small pieces of paper? Explain this using the concept of electric charges.
homework.answerSpace: Write a short paragraph explaining the charge separation and attraction mechanism.
homework.expectedResponse: Rubbing transfers electrons from hair to comb, making comb negatively charged. This induces opposite charge on paper pieces causing attraction.

homework.title: Observing Electrostatic Attraction
homework.instructions: Perform a simple activity at home to observe electrostatic attraction.
homework.question: Rub a plastic ruler with wool or dry hair and bring it near small pieces of paper. Observe the effect. Record your observation in one sentence.
homework.visualRequirement: Draw a simple sketch of the setup (ruler, hair/wool, paper pieces).
homework.expectedResponse: The ruler attracts the paper pieces due to induced charge.

learningOutcomesCovered: Apply Coulomb's law to calculate force between point charges
Explain the fundamental properties of electric charge
Draw and interpret electric field lines
Understand the concept of electric flux
Connect theoretical concepts to real-world phenomena
